# UC-17.1.77 · Cisco ISE PSN Authentication-Per-Second (TPS) SLO and Capacity Headroom

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Each ISE box can handle a maximum number of logins per second. We watch how close each box is to its limit — and warn before it gets crowded so we can add another box.*

---

## Description

Tracks per-PSN authentication-per-second rate against the platform's published TPS ceiling. Alerts when sustained headroom falls below 30%.

## Value

PSNs that approach their TPS ceiling start dropping requests. Headroom monitoring lets capacity planning add nodes before users feel the pain — and prevents the worst-case scenario where ISE silently drops auth requests during a busy morning.

## Implementation

Schedule every 5 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Generate 6,000 authentications per minute against an SNS-3715 (5,000 TPS rated). UC-17.1.77 fires.

### Negative scenario

PSN running at 50% capacity. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5400)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count as auth_count by _time, host
| eval tps=round(auth_count/60, 1)
| lookup ise_psn_capacity.csv host OUTPUT psn_max_tps
| eval headroom_pct=round(((psn_max_tps - tps) / psn_max_tps) * 100, 1)
| where headroom_pct < 30
```

## Visualization

Per-PSN TPS gauge; headroom trend; capacity-utilization heatmap.

## Known False Positives

- Morning login storm peaks (8-9am) often dip below 30% briefly — tune.
- Maintenance on neighbor PSN concentrates load.

## References

- [Cisco SNS-3700 Series Datasheet — TPS Specs](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/collateral/security/secure-network-server/datasheet-c78-744213.html)
